Optimization Report

www.prestigeglamourgroupofcompany.com · Deployed on Vercel Free Tier

Next.js · Vercel

10,000 concurrent

Static Site

Section 01

Current State Analysis

77
Total Requests
19.4 kB
Transferred
2.3 MB
Resources
27.17 s
Finish Time
206 ms
DOMContentLoaded
🔍
Key Observation: DOMContentLoaded at 206 ms is excellent — your HTML shell loads instantly thanks to Next.js SSG/ISR. However, a 27-second finish time reveals deferred JS chunks, fonts, and unoptimized images loading lazily. The 2.3 MB resource size vs 19.4 kB transferred confirms aggressive caching is working, but the raw assets are too large and too numerous.
🔴
Reduce Unused JavaScript
Est. savings: 90 KiB
Dead JS bundles shipped to every visitor. Tree-shaking and dynamic imports needed.
🔴
Minimize Main-Thread Work
4.0 seconds blocked
JS parsing, evaluation, and layout thrashing on mobile CPUs killing TBT score.
🔴
Reduce JS Execution Time
1.8 seconds
Heavy synchronous script evaluation on page load. Break into smaller deferred chunks.
🟠
Improve Image Delivery
Mobile: 96 KiB · Desktop: 283 KiB savings
Images not using next/image properly or wrong sizes/formats served.
🟠
Render-Blocking Requests
Delaying First Paint
Fonts and non-critical CSS blocking initial render. Use font-display:swap and inline critical CSS.
🟠
Legacy JavaScript
Est. savings: 14 KiB
Polyfills shipping to modern browsers. Configure browserslist correctly.
💡
Mobile gap (81 vs 99 desktop) is almost entirely explained by: slower CPU (mobile = 4× throttle in Lighthouse), heavier JS execution, and images not sized for mobile viewports. Fix these three and you reach 95+ on mobile.
Section 02 · HIGH PRIORITY

Image Optimization — Save 283 KiB

Always use next/image — Never <img> tags
🔴 CRITICAL — 283 KiB Savings
next/image automatically serves WebP/AVIF, lazy-loads, adds width/height to prevent CLS, and generates srcsets for responsive images.
// ❌ WRONG — raw img tag, no optimization
<img src="/beach-boat.jpg" alt="boat" />

// ✅ CORRECT — next/image with all required props
import Image from 'next/image';

// For hero / above-the-fold images — PRIORITY!
<Image
  src="/beach-boat.jpg"
  alt="Prestige Glamour luxury boat"
  width={1920}
  height={1080}
  priority           // preloads this image, no lazy-load
  quality={85}       // sweet spot: quality vs size
  sizes="(max-width: 768px) 100vw, (max-width: 1200px) 80vw, 1920px"
/>

// For below-fold images — let them lazy load
<Image
  src="/gallery-item.jpg"
  alt="Gallery"
  width={800}
  height={600}
  loading="lazy"     // default, explicit for clarity
  quality={75}
  placeholder="blur"
  blurDataURL="data:image/jpeg;base64,..."
/>
Configure next.config.js for AVIF + WebP
🔴 CRITICAL — Up to 50% smaller than WebP
// next.config.js
const nextConfig = {
  images: {
    formats: ['image/avif', 'image/webp'], // AVIF first — 50% smaller
    minimumCacheTTL: 31536000,           // 1 year cache
    deviceSizes: [640, 750, 828, 1080, 1200, 1920],
    imageSizes: [16, 32, 48, 64, 96, 128, 256, 384],
    dangerouslyAllowSVG: false,
    contentSecurityPolicy: "default-src 'self'; script-src 'none'; sandbox;",
  },
  // Enable gzip + brotli compression
  compress: true,
};

module.exports = nextConfig;
Pre-optimize source images before upload
🟠 IMPORTANT — Reduce source file size
next/image cannot compress beyond the source. Start with optimized originals.
# Install Sharp (required for Vercel image optimization)
npm install sharp

# Use squoosh-cli or imagemin for batch processing
npm install -D @squoosh/cli

# Optimize all images in /public/images/
npx @squoosh/cli --avif -d public/images/optimized public/images/*.jpg
npx @squoosh/cli --webp -d public/images/optimized public/images/*.jpg

# Target sizes:
# Hero images:     max 200 KB
# Gallery images:  max 80 KB
# Thumbnails:      max 20 KB
Section 03 · HIGH PRIORITY

JavaScript Optimization — Remove 90+ KiB

Analyze your bundle — know what you're shipping
🔴 REQUIRED FIRST STEP
# Install bundle analyzer
npm install -D @next/bundle-analyzer

// next.config.js
const withBundleAnalyzer = require('@next/bundle-analyzer')({
  enabled: process.env.ANALYZE === 'true',
});

module.exports = withBundleAnalyzer(nextConfig);

# Run analysis — opens browser with treemap
ANALYZE=true npm run build
Look for: duplicate libraries, massive icon packs loaded entirely, moment.js, lodash full builds, un-tree-shaken packages.
Dynamic imports for non-critical components
🔴 CRITICAL — Reduces initial JS parse time
import dynamic from 'next/dynamic';

// ❌ WRONG — loads ALL components upfront
import Gallery   from '@/components/Gallery';
import VideoPlayer from '@/components/VideoPlayer';
import ContactForm from '@/components/ContactForm';

// ✅ CORRECT — load only when needed
const Gallery = dynamic(() => import('@/components/Gallery'), {
  loading: () => <div className="skeleton" />,
  ssr: false, // gallery doesn't need SSR
});

const VideoPlayer = dynamic(() => import('@/components/VideoPlayer'), {
  ssr: false,
});

// Heavy UI libraries — load on interaction
const ContactForm = dynamic(() => import('@/components/ContactForm'));

// Icons — NEVER import whole library!
// ❌ import { FaInstagram, FaFacebook } from 'react-icons/fa'
// ✅ import FaInstagram from 'react-icons/fa/FaInstagram'
import FaInstagram from 'react-icons/fa/FaInstagram';
Fix Legacy JavaScript — Update browserslist
🟠 SAVES 14 KiB — Remove unnecessary polyfills
// package.json — add browserslist
{
  "browserslist": [
    "chrome >= 96",
    "firefox >= 96",
    "safari >= 15",
    "edge >= 96"
  ]
}

// next.config.js — skip old browser transforms
const nextConfig = {
  experimental: {
    optimizePackageImports: [
      'lucide-react',
      'react-icons',
      '@heroicons/react',
      'framer-motion',   // if used
    ],
  },
};
Defer heavy third-party scripts
🔴 CRITICAL — Directly reduces main-thread work
// Use next/script for ALL third-party scripts
import Script from 'next/script';

// ❌ NEVER put these in <head> directly
// <script src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js"></script>

// ✅ CORRECT — lazyOnload = loads after everything else
<Script
  src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id=G-XXXXXXXXXX"
  strategy="lazyOnload"
/>

// Analytics inline — also lazyOnload
<Script id="google-analytics" strategy="lazyOnload">{`
  window.dataLayer = window.dataLayer || [];
  function gtag(){dataLayer.push(arguments);}
  gtag('js', new Date());
  gtag('config', 'G-XXXXXXXXXX', { page_path: window.location.pathname });
`}</Script>

// Chat widgets, Intercom, HotJar — afterInteractive
<Script
  src="https://widget.example.com/widget.js"
  strategy="afterInteractive"
/>
Replace heavy libraries with lightweight alternatives
🟠 IMPORTANT — Easy wins
RemoveReplace WithSavings
moment.js (67 KB)date-fns (only import what's needed)~60 KB
lodash full (71 KB)lodash-es (tree-shakeable) or native JS~60 KB
react-icons/fa fullIndividual imports or SVG icons~40 KB
axios (11 KB)Native fetch() — built into Next.js~11 KB
jquery (87 KB)Remove entirely — use React~87 KB
Section 04 · MEDIUM PRIORITY

Font Optimization — Fix Render-Blocking

Use next/font — Zero Render Blocking, Zero Layout Shift
🔴 CRITICAL — Eliminates render-blocking font requests
Your network tab shows 3 woff2 font files being fetched from external CDN. Replace all of them with next/font.
// app/layout.tsx or pages/_app.tsx
import { Playfair_Display, Lato } from 'next/font/google';

const playfair = Playfair_Display({
  subsets: ['latin'],
  weight: ['400', '600', '700'],
  display: 'swap',           // show fallback font immediately
  preload: true,
  variable: '--font-heading',
});

const lato = Lato({
  subsets: ['latin'],
  weight: ['300', '400', '700'],
  display: 'swap',
  variable: '--font-body',
});

// Apply to root layout
export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    <html lang="en" className={`${playfair.variable} ${lato.variable}`}>
      <body>{children}</body>
    </html>
  );
}

/* globals.css */
body { font-family: var(--font-body), sans-serif; }
h1, h2, h3 { font-family: var(--font-heading), serif; }
Section 05 · CRITICAL FOR 10K USERS

Static Generation + Caching Strategy

🚀
This is your #1 weapon for 10,000 concurrent users. A purely static Next.js site on Vercel's CDN can handle millions of requests with no backend load. Every page must be pre-rendered at build time. Zero server-side work per request = infinite scalability on the free tier.
Ensure all pages use Static Generation (SSG)
🔴 CRITICAL — Serves from CDN edge, not server
// app/page.tsx — App Router (Next.js 13+)
// Force static — no dynamic server-side work
export const dynamic = 'force-static';
export const revalidate = 86400; // Revalidate once per day

export default async function HomePage() {
  // Data fetching here runs ONLY at build time
  const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/content', {
    next: { revalidate: 86400 }, // ISR: rebuild daily
  });
  return <main>...</main>;
}

// pages/ Router (older Next.js)
export async function getStaticProps() {
  return {
    props: { /* your data */ },
    revalidate: 86400, // ISR
  };
}
Add Vercel Cache-Control headers
🔴 CRITICAL — CDN caches assets for 10K users
// next.config.js
const nextConfig = {
  async headers() {
    return [
      {
        // Static assets: JS, CSS, images — 1 year immutable
        source: '/:path*.(js|css|woff2|png|jpg|jpeg|webp|avif|svg|ico)',
        headers: [
          { key: 'Cache-Control', value: 'public, max-age=31536000, immutable' },
        ],
      },
      {
        // HTML pages — short cache, revalidate on CDN
        source: '/:path*',
        headers: [
          { key: 'Cache-Control', value: 'public, s-maxage=86400, stale-while-revalidate=604800' },
          { key: 'X-Content-Type-Options', value: 'nosniff' },
          { key: 'X-Frame-Options', value: 'DENY' },
          { key: 'X-XSS-Protection', value: '1; mode=block' },
        ],
      },
    ];
  },
};
vercel.json — Edge config for maximum CDN performance
🔴 CRITICAL — Route all static assets to CDN edge
// vercel.json — place in project root
{
  "framework": "nextjs",
  "buildCommand": "next build",
  "outputDirectory": ".next",
  "headers": [
    {
      "source": "/_next/static/(.*)",
      "headers": [
        { "key": "Cache-Control", "value": "public, max-age=31536000, immutable" }
      ]
    },
    {
      "source": "/images/(.*)",
      "headers": [
        { "key": "Cache-Control", "value": "public, max-age=31536000, immutable" }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "rewrites": [],
  "regions": ["sin1"]  // Singapore — closest to Sri Lanka
}
Section 06 · MEDIUM PRIORITY

Critical CSS + Render Path Optimization

Inline Critical CSS — Eliminate render-blocking stylesheets
🟠 IMPORTANT — Fixes render-blocking insights flag
// Install critters for automatic critical CSS inlining
npm install critters

// next.config.js
const nextConfig = {
  experimental: {
    optimizeCss: true, // Inlines critical CSS automatically
  },
};

/* For Tailwind CSS — purge aggressively */
// tailwind.config.js
module.exports = {
  content: [
    './pages/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx,mdx}',
    './components/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx,mdx}',
    './app/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx,mdx}',
  ],
  // Remove ALL unused utilities at build time
};
Optimize metadata & preload hints in layout
🟠 IMPORTANT — LCP improvement
// app/layout.tsx
export const metadata = {
  title: 'Prestige Glamour Group of Company',
  description: 'Your description here',
};

export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    <html lang="en">
      <head>
        {/* Preconnect to external origins you NEED */}
        <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
        <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossOrigin="anonymous" />

        {/* DNS-prefetch for analytics */}
        <link rel="dns-prefetch" href="https://www.google-analytics.com" />

        {/* Preload hero image for LCP */}
        <link
          rel="preload"
          as="image"
          href="/images/hero.webp"
          type="image/webp"
        />
      </head>
      <body>{children}</body>
    </html>
  );
}
Section 07 · ARCHITECTURE

10,000 Concurrent Users on Vercel Free Tier

⚠️
Vercel Free Tier limits: 100 GB bandwidth/month · 1,000 serverless function invocations/day · 6,000 build minutes/month. For 10K concurrent users, you must eliminate all serverless function calls. Every request must be served from CDN edge cache only.
Full Static Export — The ultimate free tier strategy
🔴 ESSENTIAL FOR 10K USERS FREE
Export your entire Next.js site as static HTML files. Vercel serves them from CDN globally with no function invocations.
// next.config.js — enable static export
const nextConfig = {
  output: 'export',              // generates /out folder
  trailingSlash: true,          // /about/ instead of /about
  skipTrailingSlashRedirect: true,
  images: {
    unoptimized: false,         // keep next/image optimization via Vercel
  },
};

# Build and preview locally
npm run build
npx serve out

# IMPORTANT: Remove these features (incompatible with static export):
# ❌ API routes (/api/*)
# ❌ getServerSideProps
# ❌ middleware that modifies responses
# ✅ getStaticProps — works perfectly
# ✅ getStaticPaths — works perfectly
# ✅ ISR via revalidate — works on Vercel
Bandwidth math — will your free tier survive?
🔴 MUST CALCULATE BEFORE LAUNCH
ScenarioPage SizeSessions/DayMonthly BandwidthFree Tier
Conservative300 KB5,000~45 GB✓ OK
10K peak (1h)300 KB10,000~90 GB⚠ Near limit
Viral traffic300 KB50,000~450 GB✗ Upgrade needed
💡
Target page size under 200 KB (after all optimizations). This gives you comfortable headroom for 10K daily visitors on the free tier. Current 2.3 MB resources is way too large — but cached assets won't re-download, so effective payload per return visitor is near zero.
Contact forms without serverless functions
🟠 IMPORTANT — Replaces API routes
Static sites can't use /api routes under high load without hitting Vercel function limits. Use these free alternatives:
ServiceFree TierHow to use
Formspree50 submissions/monthaction="https://formspree.io/f/YOUR_ID"
Web3Forms250/monthfetch('https://api.web3forms.com/submit')
Netlify Forms100/monthnetlify attribute on form
EmailJS200/monthemailjs.send() from client
 
// Example: Web3Forms (generous free tier)
const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  const response = await fetch('https://api.web3forms.com/submit', {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      access_key: 'YOUR_KEY',
      name: formData.name,
      email: formData.email,
      message: formData.message,
    }),
  });
};
Section 08 · ACCESSIBILITY

Accessibility — 96 → 100 (Fix 2 Remaining Issues)

Common fixes for the missing 2 accessibility points
🔵 QUICK WINS
/* Fix 1: Color contrast — ensure 4.5:1 ratio for text */
/* Use: https://webaim.org/resources/contrastchecker/ */
.text-light-on-light {
  color: #1a1a1a; /* Never use gray text on white bg below 4.5:1 */
}

/* Fix 2: Every image must have meaningful alt text */
/* Fix 3: All form inputs need aria-labels */
<input
  type="email"
  aria-label="Email address"
  placeholder="Enter your email"
/>

/* Fix 4: Skip navigation link for keyboard users */
<a href="#main-content" className="sr-only focus:not-sr-only">
  Skip to main content
</a>

/* Fix 5: Landmark roles */
<header role="banner">...</header>
<main   id="main-content">...</main>
<nav    aria-label="Main navigation">...</nav>
<footer role="contentinfo">...</footer>
Section 09

Monitoring & CI Performance Gates

Lighthouse CI — fail builds that regress performance
🟠 IMPORTANT — Prevent regressions
# Install Lighthouse CI
npm install -D @lhci/cli

// lighthouserc.js — project root
module.exports = {
  ci: {
    collect: {
      url: ['http://localhost:3000'],
      numberOfRuns: 3,
    },
    assert: {
      preset: 'lighthouse:recommended',
      assertions: {
        'categories:performance':   ['error', { minScore: 0.90 }],
        'categories:accessibility': ['error', { minScore: 0.95 }],
        'categories:seo':           ['error', { minScore: 1.00 }],
        'first-contentful-paint':   ['error', { maxNumericValue: 1500 }],
        'interactive':              ['error', { maxNumericValue: 3500 }],
        'total-blocking-time':      ['error', { maxNumericValue: 200 }],
      },
    },
    upload: { target: 'temporary-public-storage' },
  },
};

# package.json scripts
{
  "scripts": {
    "lhci": "lhci autorun",
    "perf": "npm run build && npm run lhci"
  }
}
Vercel Speed Insights — free real user monitoring
🔵 FREE — Real user Core Web Vitals
# Install Vercel Speed Insights (free)
npm install @vercel/speed-insights

// app/layout.tsx
import { SpeedInsights } from '@vercel/speed-insights/next';

export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    <html>
      <body>
        {children}
        <SpeedInsights />  {/* Zero performance cost */}
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}

# Also add Vercel Analytics (free)
npm install @vercel/analytics

import { Analytics } from '@vercel/analytics/react';
// Add <Analytics /> next to <SpeedInsights />
Section 10 · ACTION PLAN

Implementation Priority & Effort Matrix

Priority Task Effort Impact Expected Gain
P1 — Do Now Replace all <img> with next/image + priority on hero 2h Very High +8–12 mobile score
P1 — Do Now Add output:'export' + verify all pages static 1h Very High Handles 10K users
P1 — Do Now Switch fonts to next/font (remove woff2 requests) 1h Very High Eliminates render-blocking
P1 — Do Now Move all 3rd party scripts to next/script lazyOnload 1h Very High -2s main thread work
P2 — This Week Run bundle analyzer, remove unused packages 4h High -90 KiB JS
P2 — This Week Dynamic imports for Gallery, Modal, ContactForm 2h High Faster TTI
P2 — This Week Configure cache headers in vercel.json 30min High CDN serves 10K users
P2 — This Week Pre-optimize all images (target <200KB each) 3h High -283 KiB per page load
P2 — This Week Update browserslist to modern browsers only 15min Medium -14 KiB legacy JS
P3 — Next Sprint Add optimizeCss experimental + Tailwind purge 1h Medium Smaller CSS bundle
P3 — Next Sprint Add Vercel Speed Insights + Analytics 30min Low Real user monitoring
P3 — Next Sprint Set up Lighthouse CI in GitHub Actions 2h Low Prevent regressions
P3 — Next Sprint Fix 2 remaining accessibility issues 1h Low 96 → 100 accessibility
Section 11 · TARGETS

Expected Results After All Optimizations

Metric Current (Mobile) Target (Mobile) Current (Desktop) Target (Desktop)
Performance Score 81 95+ 99 99–100
LCP ~2.8s < 1.5s ~1.2s < 1.0s
TBT (Total Blocking Time) ~400ms < 150ms ~50ms < 50ms
CLS ~0 0 ~0 0
JS Bundle Size ~2.3 MB < 800 KB ~2.3 MB < 800 KB
Accessibility 96 100 96 100
Concurrent Users (Free Tier) 10,000+ with proper static export + CDN caching

🎯 Target: Mobile 95+ · Desktop 100 · 10K Users · Zero Downtime

With the P1 optimizations alone (images, fonts, static export, script defer), you should reach Mobile 93+ within one deployment cycle. Full P1+P2 completion brings you to Mobile 95–97, Desktop 99–100 with the ability to serve 10,000 concurrent visitors from Vercel's global CDN edge — completely within the free tier for a static content website of this size.

Prestige Glamour Group — Performance Report · Senior Web Performance Audit
Generated: May 2026 · Next.js · Vercel Free Tier
